question_text: A crane lifts a steel box vertically upward with increasing speed. The rope tension is 8500 N and the weight of the box is 8000 N. Calculate the resultant force on the box and the mass of the box (use g = 10 m/s²).
explanation: First, find the resultant force: tension (up) minus weight (down) = 8500 − 8000 = 500 N upward. Then, use weight = mass × g, so mass = 8000 ÷ 10 = 800 kg. The positive resultant force confirms the box accelerates upward.
